note: Numeric table from 7 energy values [28, 69, 219, 346, 359, 408, 489]. All items are numeric and checked deterministically in Python. Optimal threshold is the minimum-misclassification candidate. B6 and B7 are open numeric answers (no fixed answer provided). B5 and B8 both expect the optimal threshold (408 for this dataset).
blocked_note: If numeric table region was not captured in OCR, mark this worksheet blocked:true and skip scoring.

WS10_B2.note: Number of midpoint threshold candidates from 7 sorted values = 6 (midpoints between each consecutive pair). Accept any integer consistent with the student's own table rows.

WS10_B4.note: Number of misclassifications at a particular threshold row.

WS10_B5.note: Threshold that yields minimum MCR for this dataset.

WS10_B6.note: No fixed answer; score 1 if counts sum to dataset size and MCR is arithmetically consistent.

WS10_B7.note: No fixed answer; score 1 if counts sum to dataset size and MCR is arithmetically consistent.

WS10_B8.note: Final optimal threshold choice. Same as B5.
